```json
{
  "diagnosis": "This HPC job exhibits severe I/O performance problems with 128 processes achieving only 3.48 MB/s total bandwidth. The primary issues are: (1) Small access granularity - despite 64KB average write size, the job performs 409,600 POSIX access operations with 384,000 unaligned accesses, indicating many small, inefficient I/O calls that create excessive syscall overhead; (2) Suboptimal file strategy - with 128 processes and high write time (6807 seconds), the application likely uses a file-per-process approach that fragments I/O and prevents collective optimizations.",

  "recommendations": [
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "bottleneck_dimension": "access_granularity",
      "explanation": "The job performs 409,600 POSIX operations with 384,000 unaligned accesses, creating massive syscall overhead. With 13.4GB written across 409,600 operations, the effective transfer size is much smaller than the reported 64KB average, indicating many sub-optimal small writes that need buffering.",
      "code_before": "for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */",
      "code_after": "/* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */\nmemcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;\nif (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }",
      "expected_speedup": "10-50x based on reducing syscall overhead",
      "kb_citation": "hacc_io_hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r1_563",
      "confidence": "high",
      "api_change": "POSIX write buffering optimization"
    },
    {
      "priority": 2,
      "bottleneck_dimension": "file_strategy",
      "explanation": "With 128 processes and 6807 seconds of write time, the application likely uses file-per-process strategy that prevents collective I/O optimizations. The high process count with individual file access creates filesystem contention and prevents bandwidth aggregation.",
      "code_before": "write(fd, buf, nbytes)  /* GenericIO.cxx, POSIX shared file */",
      "code_after": "MPI_File_write_at_all(fh, offset, buf, count, MPI_FLOAT, &status)  /* GenericIO.cxx */",
      "expected_speedup": "5-10x from collective I/O and reduced filesystem contention",
      "kb_citation": "hacc_io_hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r1_563",
      "confidence": "high",
      "api_change": "POSIX write -> MPI_File_write_at_all"
    }
  ],
  "overall_expected_improvement": "50-500x total speedup (combining access granularity buffering with collective MPI-IO strategy)"
}
```
system_prompt:
You are an HPC I/O performance expert. You analyze Darshan profiling data
and provide specific, actionable code-level optimization recommendations.

RULES:
1. Every recommendation MUST be grounded in the benchmark evidence provided.
2. Include specific before/after code snippets showing the exact change.
3. Cite the KB entry ID for each recommendation.
4. Quantify expected improvement using benchmark measurements only.
5. Do NOT fabricate performance numbers or API calls that don't exist.
6. Prioritize recommendations by expected impact (highest speedup first).
7. Use standard HPC I/O APIs: POSIX (read/write), MPI-IO (MPI_File_*), HDF5 (H5D*).
user_prompt:
Analyze this HPC job's I/O behavior and provide code-level optimization recommendations.

## Detected Bottlenecks (ML classifier, Micro-F1=0.923):
  - **access_granularity** (confidence=1.00): I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive syscall overhead
  - **file_strategy** (confidence=0.93): Suboptimal file strategy (file-per-process explosion or shared-file contention)

## Key Contributing Features (SHAP per-label attribution):

  access_granularity:
    - POSIX_FILE_NOT_ALIGNED = 384000.0000 (|SHAP|=3.9413, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_ACCESS1_COUNT = 409600.0000 (|SHAP|=1.8943, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_WRITES = 204800.0000 (|SHAP|=1.4219, increases_risk)
    - has_apmpi = 0.0000 (|SHAP|=1.2538, decreases_risk)
    - POSIX_CONSEC_READS = 204672.0000 (|SHAP|=0.7495, increases_risk)

  file_strategy:
    - nprocs = 128.0000 (|SHAP|=2.4558, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_F_WRITE_TIME = 6807.6211 (|SHAP|=1.5815, increases_risk)
    - POSIX_CONSEC_READS = 204672.0000 (|SHAP|=0.9082, decreases_risk)
    - io_active_fraction = 34.1486 (|SHAP|=0.8049, increases_risk)
    - stats_per_op = 0.0000 (|SHAP|=0.5781, increases_risk)


## Benchmark Evidence (verified Knowledge Base with source code):

  --- KB Entry 1 (ID: hacc_io_hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r1_563) ---
  Benchmark: hacc_io | Scenario: hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r1
  Bottleneck: access_granularity, file_strategy
  Source: https://github.com/ANL-CESAR/GenericIO (HACC-IO proxy) (C)
  Code (posix_shared): write(fd, buf, nbytes)  /* GenericIO.cxx, POSIX shared file */
  Code (mpiio_shared): MPI_File_write_at_all(fh, offset, buf, count, MPI_FLOAT, &status)  /* GenericIO.cxx */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:
    /* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */
memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }

  --- KB Entry 2 (ID: hacc_io_hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r2_564) ---
  Benchmark: hacc_io | Scenario: hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r2
  Bottleneck: access_granularity, file_strategy
  Source: https://github.com/ANL-CESAR/GenericIO (HACC-IO proxy) (C)
  Code (posix_shared): write(fd, buf, nbytes)  /* GenericIO.cxx, POSIX shared file */
  Code (mpiio_shared): MPI_File_write_at_all(fh, offset, buf, count, MPI_FLOAT, &status)  /* GenericIO.cxx */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:
    /* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */
memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }

  --- KB Entry 3 (ID: hacc_io_hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r3_565) ---
  Benchmark: hacc_io | Scenario: hacc_fpp_small_many_p50_n128_r3
  Bottleneck: access_granularity, file_strategy
  Source: https://github.com/ANL-CESAR/GenericIO (HACC-IO proxy) (C)
  Code (posix_shared): write(fd, buf, nbytes)  /* GenericIO.cxx, POSIX shared file */
  Code (mpiio_shared): MPI_File_write_at_all(fh, offset, buf, count, MPI_FLOAT, &status)  /* GenericIO.cxx */
  Cause: I/O operations with very small transfer sizes (<1MB), causing excessive system call overhead
  Fix: Increase transfer/buffer size to >=1MB. For POSIX: aggregate small writes into large buffers. For MPI-IO: use collective I/O with larger datatypes.
  Code BEFORE:
    for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
  Code AFTER:
    /* Buffer small writes, flush in large chunks */
memcpy(big_buf + offset, buf, 64); offset += 64;
if (offset >= 1048576) { write(fd, big_buf, offset); offset = 0; }
